title: David and Goliath
summary: A giant warrior named Goliath scared everyone in Israel. A young shepherd boy named David trusted God and stood up to him.
lessonTheme: We can be brave because God is with us.
discussionBoundaries: This story includes a battle where someone is killed. Language is kept gentle, but parents may wish to prepare younger children.

sentences.text: The Philistine army and the Israelite army faced each other on two hills.
Every day, a giant warrior named Goliath walked out and shouted at the Israelites.
Goliath was over nine feet tall and wore heavy armor from head to toe.
He shouted, "Send one man to fight me! If he wins, we will be your servants."
The Israelite soldiers were very afraid and no one wanted to fight him.
A young shepherd boy named David came to bring food to his brothers in the army.
David heard Goliath shouting and asked, "Why is everyone afraid of him?"
King Saul heard that David wanted to fight Goliath and was surprised.
David said, "God helped me protect my sheep from lions and bears. He will help me now too."
King Saul gave David heavy armor to wear, but it did not fit him.
David took it off and chose five smooth stones from a stream instead.
He walked toward Goliath with his sling and his stones.
Goliath laughed when he saw David and said, "Am I a dog, that you come at me with sticks?"
David answered, "You come with a sword, but I come in the name of the Lord."
David ran toward Goliath, swung his sling, and the stone hit Goliath in the forehead.
Goliath fell to the ground, and the Israelites cheered.
David did not win because he was the biggest or the strongest.
He won because he trusted that God was with him.

vocabulary.definition: A people who were enemies of Israel in the Bible.
A person from the nation of Israel, God's chosen people in the Old Testament.
A very tall and powerful warrior who fought for the Philistines.
A protective covering made of metal that soldiers wore in battle.
A person whose job is to watch over and care for a flock of sheep.
A simple tool made of a strap used to throw stones fast and far.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did Goliath challenge the Israelites to do every day?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: He challenged them to send one man to fight him.
comprehensionQuestions.hint: What did Goliath shout from the hill?

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did David use to fight Goliath instead of armor and a sword?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: David used five smooth stones and a sling.
comprehensionQuestions.hint: What did David pick up from the stream?

comprehensionQuestions.question: Why do you think David was not afraid even though Goliath was huge?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: David trusted that God would help him, just as God had helped him protect his sheep from wild animals.
comprehensionQuestions.hint: What did David say to King Saul about lions and bears?

comprehensionQuestions.question: Why did David say he came in the name of the Lord instead of bragging about himself?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: David's confidence came from God, not from his own strength.
comprehensionQuestions.hint: What made David different from the soldiers who were scared?

comprehensionQuestions.question: Has there ever been something that felt too big or scary for you? What helped you feel brave?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Open-ended. Look for the child connecting the idea of getting help from God or a trusted person rather than facing something alone.
